The AI Navigator Framework — A Complete Guide

The AI Navigator is an 11-step framework for building a complete AI implementation plan. It was created specifically for small and medium-sized businesses — organisations that need AI to work in practice, not in theory, and that cannot afford to waste months on experiments that go nowhere.

The framework begins with a single question: what business goal should AI serve? Every step that follows flows from the answer to that question. By the end of the process, a business holds a complete set of written documents that translate AI from an intention into an operational roadmap.

The AI Navigator framework is the methodology behind the book The AI Roadmap, written by Allan Sendagi and published in April 2025.

Take the free AI Readiness Assessment →

Why This Framework Exists

The problem is not the technology. The technology is ready. The problem is the approach most businesses take to adopting it. They start with tools. Someone signs up for ChatGPT. A manager attends a demo. A team runs experiments. Months pass. The results are scattered, the ROI is unclear, and leadership is asking what the return on all this investment actually is.

The AI Navigator framework was built to solve this problem. Its core principle is simple: don't start with a vendor, don't start with code, don't start with data. Start with direction.

Chapter 1 — Strategic Vision

<strong>What it does:</strong> Establishes the business goal that AI must serve. Forces clarity on purpose before any technology is discussed.

<strong>The tool:</strong> The Strategic Vision Canvas. Four questions answered with precision: What is the business goal? What is the specific AI use case? What outcomes do we expect? How will we measure success?

<strong>The output:</strong> Your Strategic Vision Statement — a one-page document that anchors every subsequent step.

Chapters 2–5: Analysis and Strategy

Chapter 2 (Starting Point Analysis) establishes an honest picture of where your business currently stands. Chapter 3 (Strategy Selection) produces a documented choice between Evolutionary and Revolutionary approaches. Chapter 4 (Strategy Preferences) calibrates scope, data readiness, and timeline. Chapter 5 (Workflow Analysis) identifies which workflows are worth improving with AI — and which are not.

Chapters 6–9: Design and Tool Selection

Chapter 6 (Task Deconstruction) scores each task on predictability, data availability, decision complexity, and frequency. Chapter 7 (AI Task Canvas) turns good ideas into precise build briefs. Chapter 8 (Process Mapping) creates visual blueprints of AI-integrated workflows. Chapter 9 (Tool Identification) uses the 2×2 AI Functionality Matrix to identify tool categories before any vendor is evaluated.

Chapters 10–11: Execution

Chapter 10 (Implementation Plan) translates everything into a phased, operational project plan with objectives, tasks, timelines, budgets, owners, metrics, and risks. Chapter 11 (Execution and Iteration) initiates Phase 1 and establishes the governance and monitoring framework that ensures the AI system improves over time.

What You Hold at the End

A business that completes the AI Navigator framework holds eleven specific documents. They are not generic templates. They are built around your business, your workflows, your data, and your goals. Together they constitute your AI Playbook — usable independently of any consultant, by any development team, with any future partner.

Frequently Asked Questions

Ready to assess your AI readiness?

The AI Readiness Assessment evaluates your business across five dimensions — workflow type, decision complexity, data availability, process volume, and team size — and gives you a personalised score with specific recommended next steps. It takes three minutes.

Take the free AI Readiness Assessment →

Allan Sendagi is the author of The AI Roadmap and founder of SafeHaven AI. He works with SMEs across Qatar and the GCC to build structured AI implementation plans using the AI Navigator framework.
